item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The moment I realized what this was, I started laughing my ass off because I couldn’t help but think of that one meme image of Kermit the Frog with a gun. I wouldn’t be surprised if Three Moons Murderer Jim James Henson actually did make an instance of SCP-4922 like that.

Take my +1, you magnificent bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Turning my childhood hero into a murderous army colonel making danger puppets is hilarious +1

Slight nitpick: article says the only way to destroy the instances is heavy machine gun fire… but wouldn’t explosives or other massive damage weapons do the trick?

item.title: Okapi
item.content:encoded: While it was funny, why is Jim Henson poaching endangered species?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: they already contained four instances, and all emerging ones are hostile and dangerous. there is no point on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
remove the part about machine gun fire to destroy instances
the goal is to contain, not destroy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the last line shouldn't it be SCP-4922-4 self destructs instead of SCP-4922-PRIME since there is no SCP-4922-PRIME, only PoI-4922. Plus it was the body that self-destructed and the body is referred to as SCP-4922-4.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last line killed me (no pun intended). I really enjoy reading the 3 Moons Initiative skips, there should be enough of them soon if not already to have it’s own tag. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looks like we’re gonna need a bigger bird. +1

Also naming it The Rainbow Connection was a nice touch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i think that a line is missing between the introductory sentance of the description and the listing of traits. otherwise quite good

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

Any last requests? Like for a cup of Wilkins coffee?

No.

You don't drink Wilkins?

No.

How shocking!

I feel like this piece could use with a bit more tightening; sentences like 'Indeed, the whimsical designs…' feel off to me, tonally. In addition, this article relies heavily on Three Moon lore — a lot of its components don't work unless you're familiar with its context. A little less of that could have made this more accessible.

All that being said, the fundamental premise (weaponized Jim Henson puppets) is a great one. What makes this especially fun is that you dodged the easy Five Nights at Freddy's parallel and instead went with a much better Sesame Street meets the A-Team/Platoon premise.

I was on the edge of no-vote until that final bit in the interview. There's a certain elegant hilarity to subverting the finality of death like that.
